app.name: sqz
app.one_liner: Compress LLM context to save tokens and reduce costs

screens.name: Main Compression Interface
screens.purpose: Display real-time token savings and compression stats for active LLM sessions

rust_commands.name: compress
rust_commands.purpose: Compress LLM context by applying deduplication and lightweight referencing

rust_commands.name: init
rust_commands.purpose: Initialize sqz configuration for supported agents and tools

rust_commands.name: version
rust_commands.purpose: Display current version of sqz CLI

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement core deduplication engine in Rust
week 1: Integrate with VS Code and Firefox extensions
acceptance_tests: Given repeated file reads in a session, sqz returns full content on first read and ~13-token reference on subsequent reads
Installation succeeds on Linux via install.sh and Windows via cargo or install.ps1
MCP initialize response correctly advertises non-empty tool capabilities
sqz init supports opencode.jsonc configuration format
